homeMsg: Design a new activity to do with your course, or launch an activity based on a design. You can also share an activity with your colleagues and search activities shared by others.
import_phrase1: Each user have to use one line: First the email address (required), then the password (required),
import_phrase2: then the name (optional), at last the last-name (optional). Every field have to be separated by comma

justify_ranking_item_text: this item, regardless of the position in which it is ranked

account_already_exists: An account already exists with this email.
email_doesnt_exist: The requested email address is not registered.
teacher_account_request_sent: Your request to upgrade to a teacher account has been sent. Please allow some time for your request to be processed. You will be contacted via email.

duplicate_teacher_account_request: There is already another teacher account request on your behalf in our records. Please wait for it to complete.

designContext: You can search for designs created by other users in the list below, and import them into your collection of own designs.
giveDesignLaunch: Give a description to the activity
chooseDesignLaunch: Here you can choose an activity design to work with your students on EthicApp.
launchActivityPanel1: You can launch an activity design as many times as you require.
launchActivityPanel2: When a design is launched, EthicApp generates an access code that you can give to your students to join in to participate.
designsPanel1: You can create a new design, or continue editing a design of yours that is free for editing.
designsPanel2: You can also import a design shared by another user to add to your designs collection.
newDesignsPanel1: In this activity each question is accompanied by a numerical scale of 2 to 10 values, and a box to enter a comment.
newDesignsPanel2: The scale must include poles described by opposite terms, for example “Fair” - “Unfair”, “Prudent” - “Imprudent”.
SemanticDescription: Semantic Differential: It allows for questions about the case in which students make their ethical judgment using numeric scales that relate opposite terms, for example, Fair-Unfair, Responsible-Irresponsible, etc. The scales can range from dichotomous (two values) up to ten values.
RankingDescription: Ranking: It allows for questions about the case in which students must rank the actors based on a question regarding an ethical criterion. For example, "Rank the actors by placing first the one you believe acted in the most prudent manner".
confirmationMessage: Are you sure you want to finish the activity?
access_denied_explanation: You do not have permission to access this page.
resource_not_found_explanation: Sorry, the page you are looking for does not exist.
internal_server_error_explanation: Something went wrong, please try again later.
go_back: Go Back to the Previous Page
